what I need to do next.

content.content:
- content: 'Account recovery should be handled according to the current process state.

    Different recovery stages may require different kinds of user participation,

    so support should not assume that the entire recovery process must be handed

    to the user before the current stage is identified.


    When a user completes a required checkpoint outside the assistant''s direct

    control, re-check the current process state before continuing. If the case

    has returned to a non-sensitive support stage, assistance may resume from

    that state rather than ending the whole recovery task.

    '

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Troubleshoot Your Account | Tesla Support

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/troubleshoot-account

  - source: Tesla

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # Troubleshoot Your Account


  If you are having trouble signing in to your Tesla Account, choose the action that
  matches the sign-in issue you are experiencing.


  ## Need to Create an Account


  If you do not already have a Tesla Account, create one to access your profile and
  Tesla products.


  ## Forgot Your Password


  If you have forgotten your password, use Tesla''s password-reset process. You will
  need access to the primary email address on the account or to a recovery email address
  associated with the account.


  ## Lost Access to Your Authenticator App


  If you cannot sign in because the authenticator app is lost or inaccessible, contact
  Tesla Customer Support.


  ## Can''t Access Your Sign-In Email


  If the primary email address is unavailable, a recovery email can be used to reset
  the password and regain access. If no recovery email is set up, contact Tesla Customer
  Support.


  The appropriate recovery action depends on the actual sign-in problem. Password
  loss, authenticator-app loss and loss of access to the sign-in email are handled
  through different recovery routes rather than one universal recovery procedure.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Troubleshoot Your Account | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/troubleshoot-account

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: How to Reset Your Tesla Account\
  \ Password | Tesla Support
- url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-to-reset-your-tesla-account-password
\
  - source: Tesla
- domain: tesla.com

## Content

# How to Reset Your Tesla\
  \ Account Password

Tesla provides separate steps for a forgotten password, loss\
  \ of access to the sign-in email address and a normal password change.

## Forgot\
  \ Password

To reset a forgotten Tesla Account password:

1. Visit Tesla's Reset\
  \ Password page.
2. Confirm the sign-in email address associated with the Tesla\
  \ Account.
3. Select the option to send a code.
4. Follow the prompts in the password-reset\
  \ flow.

## Lost Access to the Sign-In Email Address

If the primary sign-in\
  \ email address is no longer accessible, a recovery email address associated with\
  \ the Tesla Account can be used:

1. Visit the Reset Password page.
2. Confirm\
  \ the sign-in email address and continue.
3. Select “Verify Another Way.”\
  
4. Choose the recovery email.
5. Follow the prompts.

## Change a Known Password
\
  
An account holder who can sign in may change the password from the Tesla website\
  \ or from the Tesla app. On the website, open Profile Settings and select the password\
  \ reset option. In the Tesla app, open the account menu, go to Security and Privacy,\
  \ open Security, sign in, and use the password control under Sign In Management.
\
  
If the account holder is still unable to reset the password, Tesla directs the\
  \ user to contact Customer Support.
"
metadata: {}
title: How to Reset Your Tesla Account Password | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-to-reset-your-tesla-account-password

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: How to Add or Remove Vehicles\
  \ in the Tesla App | Tesla Support
- url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app
\
  - source: Tesla
- domain: tesla.com

## Content

# How to Add or Remove Vehicles\
  \ in the Tesla App

The process for adding a vehicle depends on how the vehicle\
  \ was purchased.

A Tesla purchased directly from Tesla is automatically added\
  \ to the Tesla app. A vehicle purchased from a third party must be claimed by the\
  \ new owner.

## Transferring Ownership

Transferring ownership gives the new\
  \ owner access to Tesla app features and removes vehicle information and access\
  \ from the previous owner. Tesla states that each vehicle can have one owner and\
  \ one Tesla Account at a time.

For a pre-owned vehicle, the new owner may need\
  \ to provide personal identification and proof of ownership. Acceptable examples\
  \ listed by Tesla include government-issued identification and documents such as\
  \ registration, title or a bill of sale. When required ownership information is\
  \ verified, the vehicle ownership can be transferred in the Tesla app.

## Claiming\
  \ a Vehicle Purchased from a Third Party

To claim ownership in the Tesla app:
\
  
1. Open the Tesla app.
2. Open the menu.
3. Select “My Products,”\
  \ then “Add Product.”
4. Follow the prompts.

The claimant may be asked\
  \ for the vehicle identification number, ownership documents and owner information.\
  \ Tesla notes that the request can take up to 48 hours to process.

## Removing\
  \ or Transferring a Vehicle

A vehicle should be removed from an account only\
  \ when the account holder no longer owns it or no longer needs access. Removing\
  \ or transferring a vehicle can affect Tesla app access, subscriptions and benefits\
  \ associated with the account.
"
metadata: {}
title: How to Add or Remove Vehicles in the Tesla App | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app

content.content: I need user clarification or authorization before continuing, so I will stop here until the user responds.

Here’s what I found:

- Your **2021 Tesla Model 3 is still linked to your account**, with active vehicle access and active ownership.
- There are **no active vehicle-service or account-verification workflows** recorded, so this does not currently look like a vehicle-connection or ownership problem.
- No recoverable account-recovery session was found in the support system, even after you completed the Tesla authentication checkpoint. I therefore can’t advance that recovery session from here.

### What to do next

1. **Return to the official Tesla sign-in flow** and continue from the screen where you completed verification. Do not share your password or verification code.
2. If the issue is a forgotten password, use Tesla’s **Reset Password** page and select the email-code option.
3. If you cannot access your primary sign-in email, choose **“Verify Another Way”** and use the recovery email, if one is configured.
4. If the problem is a lost authenticator app or no recovery email is available, contact **Tesla Customer Support**.
5. Once signed in, check the Tesla app’s vehicle list. Since the vehicle remains active and linked in the account records, first try signing out and back in, updating the app, and verifying that you used the same Tesla account email. Do **not** remove or transfer the vehicle unless you no longer own it.

Tesla’s relevant guidance:

- [Troubleshoot Your Account](https://www.tesla.com/support/troubleshoot-account)
- [Reset Your Tesla Account Password](https://www.tesla.com/support/how-to-reset-your-tesla-account-password)
- [Add or Remove Vehicles in the Tesla App](https://www.tesla.com/support/how-add-or-remove-vehicles-tesla-app)
